introSection: Blue Flash is a steel roller coaster designed and built by John Ivers, located in Pataskala, Ohio, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content: The ride began development in 2000, and was completed in roughly 2005, and was opened to the public. A second coaster, named "Blue Too" had also been developed by Ivers and was built in close proximity to the original. In late 2019 Ivers announced that he would no longer be operating the coaster. In 2020 the ride was bought by the Haunted Hoochie in Pataskala, Ohio, but it is said to not currently operate.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The train is nothing more than an office chair with a few modifications to attach wheels designed for the track. It also has a seatbelt to hold riders in.

fullText:
Blue Flash is a steel roller coaster designed and built by John Ivers, located in Pataskala, Ohio, USA.

The ride began development in 2000, and was completed in roughly 2005, and was opened to the public. A second coaster, named "Blue Too" had also been developed by Ivers and was built in close proximity to the original. In late 2019 Ivers announced that he would no longer be operating the coaster. In 2020 the ride was bought by the Haunted Hoochie in Pataskala, Ohio, but it is said to not currently operate.



The train is nothing more than an office chair with a few modifications to attach wheels designed for the track. It also has a seatbelt to hold riders in.
